senses.gloss: to eulogize, to pay homage to a dead person, to commemorate the times of
to be on the tracks of, to follow the traces of
to watch, to observe, to wait for
to stick, to stay, to remain

examples.text:
يَا كَرَوَانًا ضُكَّ فَأَكْبَأَنَّا
فَشَنَّ بِٱلسَلْخِ فَلَمَّا شَنَّا … بَلَّ ٱلذُنَابَى عَبَسًا مُبِنَّا
examples.english: You thick-knee! Which when bumped will clew and toss dung and having hurled … moistens the tail with sticky soil!

etymology.text:
Denominal verb of إِبَّان (ʔibbān, “time”).

Denominal verb of بَنَّة (banna, “odour in particular from lodging-places due to cattle”), or a previous meaning “ground” of بُنّ (bunn), Omani Arabic بُون (būn, “origin, root”).
